zone_section_start.title_label_raw: Dartmouth Land Use By-law Page 96 PART 29: CD-2 (C&D MATERIALS PROCESSING FACILITIES) ZONE (RC-Sep

zone_class_reference.summary: CD-2 is an established class of use zone.

prohibitions.summary: CD-2 USES PERMITTED No development permit shall be issued in any CD-2 (C&D Recycling) Zone except for the following, pursuant to the Site Plan Approval process: - Construction and Demolition Materials Processing Facilities - All CD-1 Zone uses - Uses Accessory to permitted uses, excluding construction and demolition disposal - Accessory dwelling unit which are provided for the purposes of safety, security, or maintenance

CD-2 ZONE REQUIREMENTS In any CD -2 Zone, no development permit shall be issued except in conformity with the following: Minimum Lot Area 3,716 square metres (40,000 square feet) central services 11,148 square metres (120,000 square feet) on-site services Minimum Frontage 15 metres (49.2 feet) central services

OTHER REQUIREMENTS: C&D MATERIALS TRANSFER STATIONS In any CD-2 Zone, no development permit shall be issued for any C&D Materials Transfer Stations except in conformity with the provision for such a use as contained within the CD-1 Zone.

OTHER REQUIREMENTS: C&D MATERIALS PROCESSING FACILITIES No development permit shall be issued for C&D materials processing facility except in compliance with the following provisions: (a) any building, structure or area used for processing shall meet the following separation distances: (i) from any property line 60 metres (196.8 feet) (ii) from the nearest residential dwelling or institutional use 90 metres (295.3 feet) (iii) from a watercourse 60 metres (196.8 feet) Dartmouth Land Use By-law Page 97 (b) notwithstanding Section 53(C)(4)(a), where a building or structure is not to be located within 250 metres of a residential or institutional use or building, the building setback from any property line may be reduced to

GENERAL REQUIREMENTS: C&D MATERIALS OPERATIONS No development permit shall be issued for a C&D Materials Operation except in compliance with the following provisions: (a) the operation complies with the general zone requirements as outlined in Section 53(B)(4). (b) notwithstanding Sections 53(C)(2) to 53(C)(4), inclusive, more than one C&D Materials operation is permitted on a site and each use shall be subject to the applicable standards unless the uses are not clearly differentiated than the more stringent require ments shall apply to the permitted uses.

dimensional_controls.text: metres (98.4 feet) on-site services Minimum Front Yard 30 metres (98.4 feet) Minimum Side Yard 30 metres (98.4 feet) Minimum Rear Yard 30 metres (98.4 feet) Maximum Lot Coverage 50 % Maximum Height 11 metres (36.0 feet)

notwithstanding Section 53(C)(4)(a), any C&D Materials Processing Facility which is to be totally enclosed within a building (no outdoor storage of material, product, processing area, or equipment) setback from any property line may be reduced to 10 metres (32.8 feet).

other_requirements.text: GENERAL REQUIREMENTS: SITE PLAN APPROVAL C&D Materials Operations are subject to approval of a site plan. The Development Officer shall approve a site plan for each use which deals with those matters outlined in Section 53(B)(5).
